agentResultText:
All done. Here's what was implemented:

**Auth infrastructure:**
- `src/lib/auth.ts` — NextAuth config with `CredentialsProvider` (email/password via bcrypt)
- `src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts` — NextAuth API route
- `src/app/api/register/route.ts` — Registration endpoint (validates input, hashes password, creates user)
- `src/middleware.ts` — Protects `/dashboard` and `/settings` routes (redirects to login)
- `src/types/next-auth.d.ts` — Type augmentation to include `user.id` in session

**UI:**
- `src/app/login/page.tsx` — Sign in form with error handling
- `src/app/register/page.tsx` — Sign up form (auto-signs in after registration)
- `src/components/navbar.tsx` — Auth-aware navbar (shows sign in/up when logged out, user name + sign out when logged in)
- `src/components/session-provider.tsx` — Client-side session wrapper

**Updated existing pages:**
- Dashboard now shows only the authenticated user's posts
- Settings now edits the authenticated user's profile
- Server actions verify the session before mutating data

**Schema & seed:**
- Added `password` field to User model
- Seed creates admin user with password `password123`

**To finish setup**, run the migration against your database:
```
npx prisma migrate dev --name add-user-password
npx prisma db seed
```

1. **Added `/signup` page** — the test expects sign-up at `/signup`, so I created the page there (the old `/register` page still works too)
2. **Made `name` field optional** — the test only fills email and password during sign-up; the name now defaults to the email username if not provided
3. **Updated internal links** — navbar and login page now link to `/signup`

agentStdoutHead:
{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":120214,"duration_api_ms":105432,"num_turns":20,"result":"Build passes. Here's what was added:

**Changes:**
- `prisma/schema.prisma` — `password` is now optional (`String?`) so Google OAuth users don't need one
- `src/lib/auth.ts` — Added `GoogleProvider` + `signIn` callback that auto-creates a database user on first Google sign-in
- `src/app/login/page.tsx` — "Sign in with Google" button below the email/password form
- `src/app/signup/page.tsx` — "Sign up with Google" button below the registration form
- `.env` — Added `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` placeholders

**To enable Google OAuth**, fill in the credentials in `.env` from the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials):
- Create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID
- Set the authorized redirect URI to `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/callback/google`","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"35dd5ca9-3cd2-415e-8515-db8...
agentResultText:
Build passes. Here's what was added:

**Changes:**
- `prisma/schema.prisma` — `password` is now optional (`String?`) so Google OAuth users don't need one
- `src/lib/auth.ts` — Added `GoogleProvider` + `signIn` callback that auto-creates a database user on first Google sign-in
- `src/app/login/page.tsx` — "Sign in with Google" button below the email/password form
- `src/app/signup/page.tsx` — "Sign up with Google" button below the registration form
- `.env` — Added `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` placeholders

**To enable Google OAuth**, fill in the credentials in `.env` from the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials):
- Create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID
- Set the authorized redirect URI to `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/callback/google`

promptSent: Add a sign-out button to the header that works across all pages. Handle session expiry gracefully.
agentStdoutHead:
{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":10970,"duration_api_ms":10941,"num_turns":2,"result":"Already in place:

- **Sign Out button** (line 35-41) — visible on all pages when authenticated, calls `signOut({ callbackUrl: "/" })` to redirect to home after sign-out
- **Session expiry** — handled by NextAuth's `SessionProvider` and the middleware. The `useSession` hook automatically detects expired sessions and updates the UI. The middleware redirects expired sessions to `/login` on protected routes. Server components also check `getServerSession` and redirect if null.

No changes needed.","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"35dd5ca9-3cd2-415e-8515-db8a68cb8652","total_cost_usd":0.42503425,"usage":{"input_tokens":4,"cache_creation_input_tokens":60787,"cache_read_input_tokens":77891,"output_tokens":246,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":60787,"epheme...
agentResultText:
Already in place:

- **Sign Out button** (line 35-41) — visible on all pages when authenticated, calls `signOut({ callbackUrl: "/" })` to redirect to home after sign-out
- **Session expiry** — handled by NextAuth's `SessionProvider` and the middleware. The `useSession` hook automatically detects expired sessions and updates the UI. The middleware redirects expired sessions to `/login` on protected routes. Server components also check `getServerSession` and redirect if null.

No changes needed.

promptSent: Add organization support so users can belong to a team and only see data for their organization.
agentStdoutHead:
{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":179709,"duration_api_ms":158010,"num_turns":32,"result":"Done. Here's what was added:

**Schema** — New `Organization` model with `id`, `name`, timestamps. `User` and `Post` both get an optional `orgId` foreign key.

**Auth flow** — When a user registers (email/password or Google OAuth), an organization is automatically created for them. The `orgId` is stored in the JWT and exposed via `session.user.orgId`.

**Data scoping:**
- Dashboard queries posts by `orgId` — team members in the same org see all org posts
- Post creation stamps the `orgId` on new posts
- Update/delete actions verify the post belongs to the user's org
- An "Author" column shows who wrote each post

**Settings page** — New "Organization" card showing:
- Editable org name
- List of team members (name and email)","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"35dd5ca9-3cd2-415e-8515-db8a68cb8652","total_cost_usd":1.77186575,"usage":{"input_t...

agentResultText:
Done. Here's what was added:

**Schema** — New `Organization` model with `id`, `name`, timestamps. `User` and `Post` both get an optional `orgId` foreign key.

**Auth flow** — When a user registers (email/password or Google OAuth), an organization is automatically created for them. The `orgId` is stored in the JWT and exposed via `session.user.orgId`.

**Data scoping:**
- Dashboard queries posts by `orgId` — team members in the same org see all org posts
- Post creation stamps the `orgId` on new posts
- Update/delete actions verify the post belongs to the user's org
- An "Author" column shows who wrote each post

**Settings page** — New "Organization" card showing:
- Editable org name
- List of team members (name and email)
